g.text: guards against: a crate that LOOKS verified because each file went green, but
where the base loop strengthened a contract for local convenience that is
globally or guidance-wrong (e.g. specified network behaviour the guidance said
Bridge -> a creusot-sl instance (run as a sub-agent, one per file)
Mission guidance + scope (analyze crate xxx in dir yyy; e.g. 'specify thoroughly; disregard network access')
The system's own documented intent (docstrings / README the architect reads)
Phase-0 architectural-audit claims (properties the system asserts about itself)
The crate's source (read-only, for the architect's Phase-0 audit)
Actual dependency graph (cargo metadata + module/use graph + compile)
The creusot-sl machine verdicts returned across the sub-loop boundary + cross-unit cargo creusot discharge + linked cargo test
The per-crate plan, file schedule decisions, and gate verdicts
The leaf-first topo-order refined by the audit value/risk ranking; the parallelizable-independent set
Creusot-amenability scope map (amenable vs out-of-reach: FFI/firmware/external-crate)
Value/risk ranking that focuses the schedule (e.g. attack-surface-first)
The base loop's consolidated skill / inferred-or-strengthened contract (soft output under test)
The base agent's private rationale for the skill/contract
Gate S verdict on each base skill/inferred contract (PASS / CARVE-OUT / REJECT)
The top loop's OWN learned skills (coordinator / scheduler / monitor heuristics)
catches: the base loop strengthens a contract
for LOCAL convenience in a way that is
network behaviour the guidance said to
against the guidance U and the cross-unit
oracle L the base agents do not hold
triage it does not police — via flag-rate
catches: its claims are read OFF the code
catches: a wrong topo-order (a callee verified
before it is proven, or a file silently
before its callers; no in-scope file skipped)
catches: its own editorial judgment and the
top loop's OWN learned skills — via
Endogenous overlay (dashed red border — bound read off the artifact)
